v.CNV: 那时，有谕旨从凯撒奥古士督颁发下来，叫普天下的人登记户口。
v.ESV: In those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be registered.
v.GAE: 그 때에 가이사 아구스도가 영을 내려 천하로 다 호적하라 하였으니
v.KJV: And it came to pass in those days, that there went out a decree from Caesar Augustus, that all the world should be taxed.
v.NASB: Now in those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus, that a census be taken of all the inhabited earth.
v.NIV: In those days Caesar Augustus issued a decree that a census should be taken of the entire Roman world.
v.NRSV: In those days a decree went out from Emperor Augustus that all the world should be registered.
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 아우구스투스 황제가 칙령을 내려 온 세계가 호적 등록을 하게 되었는데,
v.WLB: 그 무렵 아우구스투스 황제가 칙령을 내려 전 로마 통치 지역은 호적 등록을 하게 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 这是第一次户口登记，是在居里纽作叙利亚总督的时候举行的。
v.ESV: This was the first registration when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
v.GAE: 이 호적은 구레뇨가 수리아 총독이 되었을 때에 처음 한 것이라
v.KJV: ( And this taxing was first made when Cyrenius was governor of Syria.)
v.NASB: This was the first census taken while Quirinius was governor of Syria.
v.NIV: (This was the first census that took place while Quirinius was governor of Syria.)
v.NRSV: This was the first registration and was taken while Quirinius was governor of Syria.
v.SAENEW: 이 첫 번째 호적 등록은 구레뇨가 시리아의 총독으로 있을 때에 시행한 것이다.
v.WLB: 이것은 구레뇨가 시리아의 총독으로 있을 때 실시된 첫 번째 호적 등록이었습니다.

v.CNV: 众人各归各城去登记户口。
v.ESV: And all went to be registered, each to his own town.
v.GAE: 모든 사람이 호적하러 각각 고향으로 돌아가매
v.KJV: And all went to be taxed, every one into his own city.
v.NASB: And everyone was on his way to register for the census, each to his own city.
v.NIV: And everyone went to their own town to register.
v.NRSV: All went to their own towns to be registered.
v.SAENEW: 모든 사람이 호적 등록을 하러 저마다 자기 고향으로 갔다.
v.WLB: 그래서 모든 사람은 호적을 등록하기 위해 각각 자기 고향으로 갔습니다.

v.CNV: 约瑟本是大卫家族的人，也从加利利的拿撒勒上犹太去，到了大卫的城伯利恒，
v.ESV: And Joseph also went up from Galilee, from the town of Nazareth, to Judea, to the city of David, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and lineage of David,
v.GAE: 요셉도 다윗의 집 족속이므로 갈릴리 나사렛 동네에서 유대를 향하여 베들레헴이라 하는 다윗의 동네로
v.KJV: And Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judaea, unto the city of David, which is called Bethlehem; (because he was of the house and lineage of David:)
v.NASB: Joseph also went up from Galilee, from the city of Nazareth, to Judea, to the city of David which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David,
v.NIV: So Joseph also went up from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to Bethlehem the town of David, because he belonged to the house and line of David.
v.NRSV: Joseph also went from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to the city of David called Bethlehem, because he was descended from the house and family of David.
v.SAENEW: 요셉은 다윗 가문의 자손이므로, 갈릴리의 나사렛 동네에서 유대에 있는 베들레헴이라는 다윗의 동네로,
v.WLB: 요셉도 갈릴리 나사렛 마을을 떠나 다윗의 마을인 유대 땅 베들레헴으로 올라갔습니다. 요셉은 다윗 가문의 직계 혈통이었기 때문입니다.

v.CNV: 与所聘之妻马利亚一同登记户口。那时马利亚的身孕已经重了。
v.ESV: to be registered with Mary, his betrothed, who was with child.
v.GAE: 그 약혼한 마리아와 함께 호적하러 올라가니 마리아가 이미 잉태하였더라
v.KJV: To be taxed with Mary his espoused wife, being great with child.
v.NASB: in order to register along with Mary, who was engaged to him, and was with child.
v.NIV: He went there to register with Mary, who was pledged to be married to him and was expecting a child.
v.NRSV: He went to be registered with Mary, to whom he was engaged and who was expecting a child.
v.SAENEW: 자기의 약혼자인 마리아와 함께 등록하러 올라갔다. 그 때에 마리아는 임신 중이었는데,
v.WLB: 그는 약혼한 마리아와 함께 호적을 등록하러 그곳에 갔습니다. 그때 마리아는 임신 중이었습니다.

v.CNV: 他们在那里的时候，马利亚的产期到了，
v.ESV: And while they were there, the time came for her to give birth.
v.GAE: 거기 있을 그 때에 해산할 날이 차서
v.KJV: And so it was, that, while they were there, the days were accomplished that she should be delivered.
v.NASB: While they were there, the days were completed for her to give birth.
v.NIV: While they were there, the time came for the baby to be born,
v.NRSV: While they were there, the time came for her to deliver her child.
v.SAENEW: 그들이 거기에 머물러 있는 동안에, 마리아가 해산할 날이 되었다.
v.WLB: 그들이 그곳에 머무르는 동안 해산할 때가 돼

v.CNV: 生了头胎儿子，用布包着，放在马槽里，因为客店里没有地方。
v.ESV: And she gave birth to her firstborn son and wrapped him in swaddling cloths and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the inn.
v.GAE: 첫아들을 낳아 강보로 싸서 구유에 뉘었으니 이는 여관에 있을 곳이 없음이러라
v.KJV: And she brought forth her firstborn son, and wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger; because there was no room for them in the inn.
v.NASB: And she gave birth to her firstborn son; and she wrapped Him in cloths, and laid Him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.
v.NIV: and she gave birth to her firstborn, a son. She wrapped him in cloths and placed him in a manger, because there was no guest room available for them.
v.NRSV: And she gave birth to her firstborn son and wrapped him in bands of cloth, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the inn.
v.SAENEW: 마리아가 첫 아들을 낳아서, 포대기에 싸서 구유에 눕혀 두었다. 여관에는 그들이 들어갈 방이 없었기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 마리아는 첫아들을 낳고는 아기를 천으로 싸서 구유에 눕혔습니다. 여관에는 그들이 들어갈 빈방이 없었기 때문입니다.

v.CNV: 在伯利恒的郊外，有一些牧人在夜间看守羊群。
v.ESV: And in the same region there were shepherds out in the field, keeping watch over their flock by night.
v.GAE: 그 지역에 목자들이 밤에 밖에서 자기 양 떼를 지키더니
v.KJV: And there were in the same country shepherds abiding in the field, keeping watch over their flock by night.
v.NASB: In the same region there were some shepherds staying out in the fields and keeping watch over their flock by night.
v.NIV: And there were shepherds living out in the fields nearby, keeping watch over their flocks at night.
v.NRSV: In that region there were shepherds living in the fields, keeping watch over their flock by night.
v.SAENEW: 그 지역에서 목자들이 밤에 들에서 지내며 그들의 양 떼를 지키고 있었다.
v.WLB: 한편 목자들은 바로 그 지역 들판에서 살며 밤에 양 떼를 지키고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 主的一位使者站在他们旁边，主的荣光四面照着他们，他们就非常害怕。
v.ESV: And an angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were filled with great fear.
v.GAE: 주의 사자가 곁에 서고 주의 영광이 그들을 두루 비추매 크게 무서워하는지라
v.KJV: And, lo, the angel of the Lord came upon them, and the glory of the Lord shone round about them: and they were sore afraid.
v.NASB: And an angel of the Lord suddenly stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them; and they were terribly frightened.
v.NIV: An angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.
v.NRSV: Then an angel of the Lord stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 주님의 한 천사가 그들에게 나타나고, 주님의 영광이 그들을 두루 비추니, 그들은 몹시 두려워하였다.
v.WLB: 주의 천사가 그들 앞에 나타났고 주의 영광이 그들을 환하게 둘러 비춰서 그들은 너무나 두려웠습니다.

v.CNV: 天使说：“不要怕！看哪！我报给你们大喜的信息，是关于万民的：
v.ESV: And the angel said to them, “Fear not, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy that will be for all the people.
v.GAE: 천사가 이르되 무서워하지 말라 보라 내가 온 백성에게 미칠 큰 기쁨의 좋은 소식을 너희에게 전하노라
v.KJV: And the angel said unto them, Fear not: for, behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy, which shall be to all people.
v.NASB: But the angel said to them, 'Do not be afraid; for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be for all the people;
v.NIV: But the angel said to them, “Do not be afraid. I bring you good news that will cause great joy for all the people.
v.NRSV: But the angel said to them, “Do not be afraid; for see — I am bringing you good news of great joy for all the people:
v.SAENEW: 천사가 그들에게 말하였다. "두려워하지 말아라. 나는 온 백성에게 큰 기쁨이 될 소식을 너희에게 전하여 준다.
v.WLB: 천사가 말했습니다. “두려워하지 마라. 보아라. 내가 모든 백성에게 큰 기쁨이 될 좋은 소식을 너희에게 알려 준다.

v.CNV: 今天在大卫的城里，为你们生了救主，就是主基督。
v.ESV: For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
v.GAE: 오늘 다윗의 동네에 너희를 위하여 구주가 나셨으니 곧 그리스도 주시니라
v.KJV: For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Saviour, which is Christ the Lord.
v.NASB: for today in the city of David there has been born for you a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
v.NIV: Today in the town of David a Savior has been born to you; he is the Messiah, the Lord.
v.NRSV: to you is born this day in the city of David a Savior, who is the Messiah, the Lord.
v.SAENEW: 오늘 다윗의 동네에서 너희에게 구주가 나셨으니, 그는 곧 그리스도 주님이시다.
v.WLB: 오늘 구주이신 주 그리스도가 다윗의 동네에서 태어나셨다.

v.CNV: 你们要找到一个婴孩，包着布，卧在马槽里，那就是记号了。”
v.ESV: And this will be a sign for you: you will find a baby wrapped in swaddling cloths and lying in a manger.”
v.GAE: 너희가 가서 강보에 싸여 구유에 뉘어 있는 아기를 보리니 이것이 너희에게 표적이니라 하더니
v.KJV: And this shall be a sign unto you; Ye shall find the babe wrapped in swaddling clothes, lying in a manger.
v.NASB: 'This will be a sign for you: you will find a baby wrapped in cloths and lying in a manger.'
v.NIV: This will be a sign to you: You will find a baby wrapped in cloths and lying in a manger.”
v.NRSV: This will be a sign for you: you will find a child wrapped in bands of cloth and lying in a manger.”
v.SAENEW: 너희는 한 갓난아기가 포대기에 싸여, 구유에 뉘어 있는 것을 볼 터인데, 이것이 너희에게 주는 표징이다."
v.WLB: 너희가 천에 싸여 구유에 누워 있는 아기를 볼 것인데 그것이 너희에게 표적이 될 것이다.”

v.CNV: 忽然有一大队天兵，同那天使一起赞美　神说：
v.ESV: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God and saying,
v.GAE: 홀연히 수많은 천군이 그 천사와 함께 하나님을 찬송하여 이르되
v.KJV: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God, and saying,
v.NASB: And suddenly there appeared with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God and saying,
v.NIV: Suddenly a great company of the heavenly host appeared with the angel, praising God and saying,
v.NRSV: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host, praising God and saying,
v.SAENEW: 갑자기 그 천사와 더불어 많은 하늘 군대가 나타나서, 하나님을 찬양하여 말하였다.
v.WLB: 갑자기 그 천사와 함께 하늘의 군대가 큰 무리를 이루며 나타나 하나님을 찬양하며 말했습니다.

v.CNV: “在至高之处，荣耀归与　神！在地上，平安归与他所喜悦的人！”
v.ESV: “Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased!”
v.GAE: 지극히 높은 곳에서는 하나님께 영광이요 땅에서는 하나님이 기뻐하신 사람들 중에 평화로다 하니라
v.KJV: Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.
v.NASB: 'Glory to God in the highest, And on earth peace among men with whom He is pleased.'
v.NIV: “Glory to God in the highest heaven, and on earth peace to those on whom his favor rests.”
v.NRSV: “Glory to God in the highest heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors!”
v.SAENEW: "더없이 높은 곳에서는 하나님께 영광이요, 땅에서는 주님께서 좋아하시는 사람들에게 평화로다."
v.WLB: “지극히 높은 곳에서는 하나님께 영광이요 땅에서는 하나님의 은총을 입는 사람들에게 평화로다.”

v.CNV: 众天使离开他们升天去了，那些牧人彼此说：“我们往伯利恒去，看看主所指示我们已经成就的事。”
v.ESV: When the angels went away from them into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let us go over to Bethlehem and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.”
v.GAE: 천사들이 떠나 하늘로 올라가니 목자가 서로 말하되 이제 베들레헴으로 가서 주께서 우리에게 알리신 바 이 이루어진 일을 보자 하고
v.KJV: And it came to pass, as the angels were gone away from them into heaven, the shepherds said one to another, Let us now go even unto Bethlehem, and see this thing which is come to pass, which the Lord hath made known unto us.
v.NASB: When the angels had gone away from them into heaven, the shepherds began saying to one another, 'Let us go straight to Bethlehem then, and see this thing that has happened which the Lord has made known to us.'
v.NIV: When the angels had left them and gone into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let’s go to Bethlehem and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has told us about.”
v.NRSV: When the angels had left them and gone into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let us go now to Bethlehem and see this thing that has taken place, which the Lord has made known to us.”
v.SAENEW: 천사들이 목자들에게서 떠나 하늘로 올라간 뒤에, 목자들이 서로 말하였다. "베들레헴으로 가서, 주님께서 우리에게 알려주신 바, 일어난 그 일을 봅시다."
v.WLB: 천사들이 떠나 하늘로 올라가자 목자들이 서로 말했습니다. “베들레헴으로 가서 주께서 우리에게 말씀하신 이 일이 정말 일어났는지 보자.”

v.CNV: 他们急忙去了，找到马利亚、约瑟和那卧在马槽里的婴孩。
v.ESV: And they went with haste and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in a manger.
v.GAE: 빨리 가서 마리아와 요셉과 구유에 누인 아기를 찾아서
v.KJV: And they came with haste, and found Mary, and Joseph, and the babe lying in a manger.
v.NASB: So they came in a hurry and found their way to Mary and Joseph, and the baby as He lay in the manger.
v.NIV: So they hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby, who was lying in the manger.
v.NRSV: So they went with haste and found Mary and Joseph, and the child lying in the manger.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 그들은 급히 달려가서, 마리아와 요셉과 구유에 누워 있는 아기를 찾아냈다.
v.WLB: 그래서 그들은 서둘러 가서 마리아와 요셉과 아기를 찾아냈습니다. 과연 아기는 구유에 누워 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 他们见过以后，就把天使对他们论这孩子的话传开了。
v.ESV: And when they saw it, they made known the saying that had been told them concerning this child.
v.GAE: 보고 천사가 자기들에게 이 아기에 대하여 말한 것을 전하니
v.KJV: And when they had seen it, they made known abroad the saying which was told them concerning this child.
v.NASB: When they had seen this, they made known the statement which had been told them about this Child.
v.NIV: When they had seen him, they spread the word concerning what had been told them about this child,
v.NRSV: When they saw this, they made known what had been told them about this child;
v.SAENEW: 그들은 이것을 보고 나서, 이 아기에 관하여 자기들이 들은 말을 사람들에게 알려 주었다.
v.WLB: 그들은 아기를 보고 나서 그 아이에 관해 들은 말을 알려 주었고

v.CNV: 听见的人，都希奇牧人所说的事。
v.ESV: And all who heard it wondered at what the shepherds told them.
v.GAE: 듣는 자가 다 목자들이 그들에게 말한 것들을 놀랍게 여기되
v.KJV: And all they that heard it wondered at those things which were told them by the shepherds.
v.NASB: And all who heard it wondered at the things which were told them by the shepherds.
v.NIV: and all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds said to them.
v.NRSV: and all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds told them.
v.SAENEW: 이것을 들은 사람들은 모두 목자들이 그들에게 전해준 말을 이상히 여겼다.
v.WLB: 그 말을 들은 모든 사람은 목자들이 한 말에 놀랐습니다.

v.CNV: 马利亚把这一切放在心里，反复思想。
v.ESV: But Mary treasured up all these things, pondering them in her heart.
v.GAE: 마리아는 이 모든 말을 마음에 새기어 생각하니라
v.KJV: But Mary kept all these things, and pondered them in her heart.
v.NASB: But Mary treasured all these things, pondering them in her heart.
v.NIV: But Mary treasured up all these things and pondered them in her heart.
v.NRSV: But Mary treasured all these words and pondered them in her heart.
v.SAENEW: 마리아는 이 모든 말을 고이 간직하고, 마음 속에 곰곰이 되새겼다.
v.WLB: 그러나 마리아만은 이 모든 일을 마음에 간직하고 곰곰이 되새겼습니다.

v.CNV: 牧人因为听见的和看见的，正像天使对他们所说的一样，就回去了，把荣耀赞美归与　神。
v.ESV: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen, as it had been told them.
v.GAE: 목자들은 자기들에게 이르던 바와 같이 듣고 본 그 모든 것으로 인하여 하나님께 영광을 돌리고 찬송하며 돌아가니라
v.KJV: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, as it was told unto them.
v.NASB: The shepherds went back, glorifying and praising God for all that they had heard and seen, just as had been told them.
v.NIV: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things they had heard and seen, which were just as they had been told.
v.NRSV: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen, as it had been told them.
v.SAENEW: 목자들은 자기들이 듣고 본 모든 일이 자기들에게 일러주신 그대로임을 알고, 돌아가면서 하나님께 영광을 돌리며 그를 찬미하였다.
v.WLB: 목자들은 자기들이 보고 들은 모든 것에 대해 하나님께 영광 돌리고 찬양하며 돌아갔습니다. 그들이 듣고 본 모든 일대로 이루어진 것입니다.

v.CNV: 满了八天，替孩子行割礼的时候，就给他起名叫耶稣，就是他成胎之前，天使所起的。
v.ESV: And at the end of eight days, when he was circumcised, he was called Jesus, the name given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
v.GAE: 할례할 팔 일이 되매 그 이름을 예수라 하니 곧 잉태하기 전에 천사가 일컬은 바러라
v.KJV: And when eight days were accomplished for the circumcising of the child, his name was called JESUS, which was so named of the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
v.NASB: And when eight days had passed, before His circumcision, His name was then called Jesus, the name given by the angel before He was conceived in the womb.
v.NIV: On the eighth day, when it was time to circumcise the child, he was named Jesus, the name the angel had given him before he was conceived.
v.NRSV: After eight days had passed, it was time to circumcise the child; and he was called Jesus, the name given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
v.SAENEW: 여드레가 차서, 아기에게 할례를 행할 때에, 그 이름을 예수라고 하였다. 그것은, 아기가 수태되기 전에, 천사가 일러준 이름이다.
v.WLB: 8일째 되는 날 할례 할 때가 되자 아기가 잉태되기 전에 천사가 일러 준 대로 그 이름을 ‘예수’라 지었습니다.

v.CNV: 满了洁净的日子，他们就按着摩西的律法，带孩子上耶路撒冷去，奉献给主。
v.ESV: And when the time came for their purification according to the Law of Moses, they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
v.GAE: 모세의 법대로 정결예식의 날이 차매 아기를 데리고 예루살렘에 올라가니
v.KJV: And when the days of her purification according to the law of Moses were accomplished, they brought him to Jerusalem, to present him to the Lord;
v.NASB: And when the days for their purification according to the law of Moses were completed, they brought Him up to Jerusalem to present Him to the Lord
v.NIV: When the time came for the purification rites required by the Law of Moses, Joseph and Mary took him to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
v.NRSV: When the time came for their purification according to the law of Moses, they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
v.SAENEW: 모세의 법대로 그들이 정결하게 되는 날이 차서, 그들은 아기를 주님께 드리려고 예루살렘으로 데리고 올라갔다.
v.WLB: 모세의 율법에 따라 정결 의식을 치를 때가 되자 요셉과 마리아는 아기를 데리고 예루살렘으로 갔습니다.

v.CNV: 正如主的律法所记：“所有头生的男孩，都当称为圣归给主。”
v.ESV: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord, “Every male who first opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord”)
v.GAE: 이는 주의 율법에 쓴 바 첫 태에 처음 난 남자마다 주의 거룩한 자라 하리라 한 대로 아기를 주께 드리고
v.KJV: (As it is written in the law of the Lord, Every male that openeth the womb shall be called holy to the Lord;)
v.NASB: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord, 'EVERY firstborn MALE THAT OPENS THE WOMB SHALL BE CALLED HOLY TO THE LORD '),
v.NIV: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord, “Every firstborn male is to be consecrated to the Lord” ),
v.NRSV: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every firstborn male shall be designated as holy to the Lord”),
v.SAENEW: 그것은 주님의 율법에 기록된 바 "어머니의 태를 처음 여는 사내아이마다, 주님의 거룩한 사람으로 불릴 것이다" 한 대로 한 것이요,
v.WLB: 이것은 주의 율법에 “첫 번째 태어나는 모든 남자 아기는 하나님께 거룩한 사람으로 불릴 것이다”라고 기록된 대로 아기를 주께 드리고

v.CNV: 又照着主的律法所说的献上祭物，就是一对斑鸠或两只雏鸽。
v.ESV: and to offer a sacrifice according to what is said in the Law of the Lord, “a pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.”
v.GAE: 또 주의 율법에 말씀하신 대로 산비둘기 한 쌍이나 혹은 어린 집비둘기 둘로 제사하려 함이더라
v.KJV: And to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.
v.NASB: and to offer a sacrifice according to what was said in the Law of the Lord, 'A PAIR OF TURTLEDOVES OR TWO YOUNG PIGEONS.'
v.NIV: and to offer a sacrifice in keeping with what is said in the Law of the Lord: “a pair of doves or two young pigeons.”
v.NRSV: and they offered a sacrifice according to what is stated in the law of the Lord, “a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons.”
v.SAENEW: 또 주님의 율법에 이르신 바 "산비둘기 한 쌍이나, 어린 집비둘기 두 마리를 드려야 한다" 한 대로, 희생제물을 드리기 위한 것이었다.
v.WLB: 또 “산비둘기 한 쌍이나 어린 집비둘기 두 마리”라고 한 주의 율법을 지켜 희생제물을 드리려는 것이었습니다.

v.CNV: 在耶路撒冷有一个人，名叫西面，这人公义虔诚，一向期待以色列的安慰者来到，又有圣灵在他身上。
v.ESV: Now there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simeon, and this man was righteous and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
v.GAE: 예루살렘에 시므온이라 하는 사람이 있으니 이 사람은 의롭고 경건하여 이스라엘의 위로를 기다리는 자라 성령이 그 위에 계시더라
v.KJV: And, behold, there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simeon; and the same man was just and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was upon him.
v.NASB: And there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon; and this man was righteous and devout, looking for the consolation of Israel; and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
v.NIV: Now there was a man in Jerusalem called Simeon, who was righteous and devout. He was waiting for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was on him.
v.NRSV: Now there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon; this man was righteous and devout, looking forward to the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 마침 예루살렘에 시므온이라는 사람이 있었는데, 그 사람은 의롭고 경건한 사람이므로, 이스라엘이 받을 위로를 기다리고 있었고, 또 성령이 그에게 임하여 계셨다.
v.WLB: 당시 예루살렘에는 시므온이라는 사람이 있었습니다. 이 사람은 의롭고 경건한 사람으로 하나님께서 이스라엘을 위로하실 날을 손꼽아 기다리고 있었습니다. 그리고 성령께서 시므온에게 머물러 계셨습니다.

v.CNV: 圣灵启示他，在死前必得见主所应许的基督，
v.ESV: And it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord's Christ.
v.GAE: 그가 주의 그리스도를 보기 전에는 죽지 아니하리라 하는 성령의 지시를 받았더니
v.KJV: And it was revealed unto him by the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.
v.NASB: And it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord's Christ.
v.NIV: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not die before he had seen the Lord’s Messiah.
v.NRSV: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Messiah.
v.SAENEW: 그는 주님께서 세우신 그리스도를 보기 전에는 죽지 아니할 것이라는 성령의 지시를 받은 사람이었다.
v.WLB: 그에게는 주의 그리스도를 보기 전에는 죽지 않으리라는 성령의 계시가 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 他又受圣灵感动进了圣殿。那时，耶稣的父母抱着孩子进来，要按着律法的规矩为他行礼。
v.ESV: And he came in the Spirit into the temple, and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him according to the custom of the Law,
v.GAE: 성령의 감동으로 성전에 들어가매 마침 부모가 율법의 관례대로 행하고자 하여 그 아기 예수를 데리고 오는지라
v.KJV: And he came by the Spirit into the temple: and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him after the custom of the law,
v.NASB: And he came in the Spirit into the temple; and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to carry out for Him the custom of the Law,
v.NIV: Moved by the Spirit, he went into the temple courts. When the parents brought in the child Jesus to do for him what the custom of the Law required,
v.NRSV: Guided by the Spirit, Simeon came into the temple; and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him what was customary under the law,
v.SAENEW: 그가 성령의 인도로 성전에 들어갔을 때에, 마침 아기의 부모가 율법이 정한 대로 행하고자 하여, 아기 예수를 데리고 들어왔다.
v.WLB: 시므온이 성령에 이끌려 성전 뜰 안으로 들어갈 때 아기의 부모가 율법의 규정대로 행하기 위해 아기 예수를 데리고 들어왔습니다.

v.CNV: 西面就把他接到手上，称颂　神说：
v.ESV: he took him up in his arms and blessed God and said,
v.GAE: 시므온이 아기를 안고 하나님을 찬송하여 이르되
v.KJV: Then took he him up in his arms, and blessed God, and said,
v.NASB: then he took Him into his arms, and blessed God, and said,
v.NIV: Simeon took him in his arms and praised God, saying:
v.NRSV: Simeon took him in his arms and praised God, saying,
v.SAENEW: 시므온이 아기를 자기 팔로 받아서 안고, 하나님을 찬양하여 말하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 시므온이 아기를 팔에 안고 하나님을 찬양하며 말했습니다.

v.CNV: “主啊，现在照你的话，释放仆人平平安安地去吧！
v.ESV: “Lord, now you are letting your servant depart in peace, according to your word;
v.GAE: 주재여 이제는 말씀하신 대로 종을 평안히 놓아 주시는도다
v.KJV: Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word:
v.NASB: 'Now Lord, You are releasing Your bond-servant to depart in peace, According to Your word;
v.NIV: “Sovereign Lord, as you have promised, you may now dismiss your servant in peace.
v.NRSV: “Master, now you are dismissing your servant in peace, according to your word;
v.SAENEW: "주님, 이제 주님께서는 주님의 말씀을 따라, 이 종을 세상에서 평안히 떠나가게 해주십니다.
v.WLB: “다스리시는 주여, 이제 주께서는 주의 종이 평안히 가게 해 주십니다.

v.CNV: 因我的眼睛已经看见你的救恩，
v.ESV: for my eyes have seen your salvation
v.GAE: 내 눈이 주의 구원을 보았사오니
v.KJV: For mine eyes have seen thy salvation,
v.NASB: For my eyes have seen Your salvation,
v.NIV: For my eyes have seen your salvation,
v.NRSV: for my eyes have seen your salvation,
v.SAENEW: 내 눈이 주님의 구원을 보았습니다.
v.WLB: 제 두 눈으로 주의 구원을 보았습니다.

v.CNV: 就是你在万民面前所预备的，
v.ESV: that you have prepared in the presence of all peoples,
v.GAE: 이는 만민 앞에 예비하신 것이요
v.KJV: Which thou hast prepared before the face of all people;
v.NASB: Which You have prepared in the presence of all peoples,
v.NIV: which you have prepared in the sight of all nations:
v.NRSV: which you have prepared in the presence of all peoples,
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 이것을 모든 백성 앞에 마련하셨으니,
v.WLB: 이 구원은 주께서 모든 백성 앞에 마련하신 것으로

v.CNV: 为要作外族人启示的光，和你民以色列的荣耀。”
v.ESV: a light for revelation to the Gentiles, and for glory to your people Israel.”
v.GAE: 이방을 비추는 빛이요 주의 백성 이스라엘의 영광이니이다 하니
v.KJV: A light to lighten the Gentiles, and the glory of thy people Israel.
v.NASB: A LIGHT OF REVELATION TO THE GENTILES, And the glory of Your people Israel.'
v.NIV: a light for revelation to the Gentiles, and the glory of your people Israel.”
v.NRSV: a light for revelation to the Gentiles and for glory to your people Israel.”
v.SAENEW: 이는 이방 사람들에게는 계시하시는 빛이요, 주님의 백성 이스라엘에게는 영광입니다."
v.WLB: 이방 사람에게는 계시의 빛이요, 주의 백성 이스라엘에게는 영광입니다.”

v.CNV: 他父母因论到他的这些话而希奇。
v.ESV: And his father and his mother marveled at what was said about him.
v.GAE: 그의 부모가 그에 대한 말들을 놀랍게 여기더라
v.KJV: And Joseph and his mother marvelled at those things which were spoken of him.
v.NASB: And His father and mother were amazed at the things which were being said about Him.
v.NIV: The child’s father and mother marveled at what was said about him.
v.NRSV: And the child’s father and mother were amazed at what was being said about him.
v.SAENEW: 아기의 아버지와 어머니는, 시므온이 아기에 대하여 하는 이 말을 듣고서, 이상하게 여겼다.
v.WLB: 아기의 아버지와 어머니는 아기에 대한 이 말에 무척 놀랐습니다.

v.CNV: 西面给他们祝福，对他母亲马利亚说：“看哪！这孩子被立，要叫以色列中许多人跌倒，许多人兴起，又要成为反对的目标，
v.ESV: And Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, “Behold, this child is appointed for the fall and rising of many in Israel, and for a sign that is opposed
v.GAE: 시므온이 그들에게 축복하고 그의 어머니 마리아에게 말하여 이르되 보라 이는 이스라엘 중 많은 사람을 패하거나 흥하게 하며 비방을 받는 표적이 되기 위하여 세움을 받았고
v.KJV: And Simeon blessed them, and said unto Mary his mother, Behold, this child is set for the fall and rising again of many in Israel; and for a sign which shall be spoken against;
v.NASB: And Simeon blessed them and said to Mary His mother, 'Behold, this Child is appointed for the fall and rise of many in Israel, and for a sign to be opposed--
v.NIV: Then Simeon blessed them and said to Mary, his mother: “This child is destined to cause the falling and rising of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be spoken against,
v.NRSV: Then Simeon blessed them and said to his mother Mary, “This child is destined for the falling and the rising of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be opposed
v.SAENEW: 시므온이 그들을 축복한 뒤에, 아기의 어머니 마리아에게 말하였다. "보십시오, 이 아기는 이스라엘 가운데 많은 사람을 넘어지게도 하고 일어서게도 하려고 세우심을 받았으며, 비방 받는 표징이 되게 하려고 세우심을 받았습니다.
v.WLB: 그러자 시므온은 그들을 축복하고 그 어머니 마리아에게 말했습니다. “보십시오. 이 아기는 이스라엘 가운데 많은 사람을 넘어지게도 하고 일어서게도 할 것이며 비난받는 표적이 되기 위해 세우심을 받았습니다.

v.CNV: （你自己的心也会被刀刺透，）这样，许多人心中的意念就要被揭露出来。”
v.ESV: (and a sword will pierce through your own soul also), so that thoughts from many hearts may be revealed.”
v.GAE: 또 칼이 네 마음을 찌르듯 하리니 이는 여러 사람의 마음의 생각을 드러내려 함이니라 하더라
v.KJV: (Yea, a sword shall pierce through thy own soul also,) that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.
v.NASB: and a sword will pierce even your own soul-- to the end that thoughts from many hearts may be revealed.'
v.NIV: so that the thoughts of many hearts will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your own soul too.”
v.NRSV: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed — and a sword will pierce your own soul too.”
v.SAENEW: -그리고 칼이 당신의 마음을 찌를 것입니다.- 그리하여 많은 사람의 마음 속 생각들이 드러나게 될 것입니다."
v.WLB: 칼이 당신의 마음도 찌를 것입니다. 그래서 이제 많은 사람의 마음속 생각이 드러나게 될 것입니다.”

v.CNV: 又有一个女先知，就是亚拿，是亚设支派法内利的女儿。她已经上了年纪，从童女出嫁，和丈夫住了七年，
v.ESV: And there was a prophetess, Anna, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was advanced in years, having lived with her husband seven years from when she was a virgin,
v.GAE: 또 아셀 지파 바누엘의 딸 안나라 하는 선지자가 있어 나이가 매우 많았더라 그가 결혼한 후 일곱 해 동안 남편과 함께 살다가
v.KJV: And there was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Aser: she was of a great age, and had lived with an husband seven years from her virginity;
v.NASB: And there was a prophetess, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was advanced in years and had lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
v.NIV: There was also a prophet, Anna, the daughter of Penuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was very old; she had lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
v.NRSV: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was of a great age, having lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
v.SAENEW: 아셀 지파에 속하는 바누엘의 딸로 안나라는 여예언자가 있었는데, 나이가 많았다. 그는 처녀 시절을 끝내고 일곱 해를 남편과 함께 살고,
v.WLB: 또 아셀 지파의 바누엘의 딸인 안나라는 여자 예언자도 있었습니다. 안나는 나이가 많았는데 결혼해서 남편과 7년 동안 살다가

v.CNV: 就寡居了，直到八十四岁（“就寡居了，直到八十四岁”或译：“就寡居了八十四年”）。她没有离开过圣殿，以禁食和祷告昼夜事奉主。
v.ESV: and then as a widow until she was eighty-four. She did not depart from the temple, worshiping with fasting and prayer night and day.
v.GAE: 과부가 되고 팔십사 세가 되었더라 이 사람이 성전을 떠나지 아니하고 주야로 금식하며 기도함으로 섬기더니
v.KJV: And she was a widow of about fourscore and four years, which departed not from the temple, but served God with fastings and prayers night and day.
v.NASB: and then as a widow to the age of eighty-four. She never left the temple, serving night and day with fastings and prayers.
v.NIV: and then was a widow until she was eighty-four. She never left the temple but worshiped night and day, fasting and praying.
v.NRSV: then as a widow to the age of eighty-four. She never left the temple but worshiped there with fasting and prayer night and day.
v.SAENEW: 과부가 되어서, 여든네 살이 되도록 성전을 떠나지 않고, 밤낮으로 금식과 기도로 하나님을 섬겨왔다.
v.WLB: 그 후 84세가 되도록 과부로 지냈습니다. 안나는 성전을 떠나지 않고 밤낮으로 금식하고 기도하면서 하나님을 섬겼습니다.

v.CNV: 就在那时候，她前来称谢　神，并且向期待耶路撒冷蒙救赎的众人，讲论孩子的事。
v.ESV: And coming up at that very hour she began to give thanks to God and to speak of him to all who were waiting for the redemption of Jerusalem.
v.GAE: 마침 이 때에 나아와서 하나님께 감사하고 예루살렘의 속량을 바라는 모든 사람에게 그에 대하여 말하니라
v.KJV: And she coming in that instant gave thanks likewise unto the Lord, and spake of him to all them that looked for redemption in Jerusalem.
v.NASB: At that very moment she came up and began giving thanks to God, and continued to speak of Him to all those who were looking for the redemption of Jerusalem.
v.NIV: Coming up to them at that very moment, she gave thanks to God and spoke about the child to all who were looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
v.NRSV: At that moment she came, and began to praise God and to speak about the child to all who were looking for the redemption of Jerusalem.
v.SAENEW: 바로 이 때에 그가 다가서서 하나님께 감사를 드리고, 예루살렘의 구원을 기다리는 모든 사람에게 이 아기에 대하여 말하였다.
v.WLB: 바로 그때 안나가 그들에게 다가와 하나님께 감사하고 예루살렘의 구원을 간절히 고대하는 모든 사람에게 그 아기에 대해 이야기했습니다.

v.CNV: 他们按着主的律法办完一切，就回加利利，到自己的城拿撒勒去了。
v.ESV: And when they had performed everything according to the Law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.
v.GAE: 주의 율법을 따라 모든 일을 마치고 갈릴리로 돌아가 본 동네 나사렛에 이르니라
v.KJV: And when they had performed all things according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city Nazareth.
v.NASB: When they had performed everything according to the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own city of Nazareth.
v.NIV: When Joseph and Mary had done everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee to their own town of Nazareth.
v.NRSV: When they had finished everything required by the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.
v.SAENEW: 아기의 부모는 주님의 율법에 규정된 모든 일을 마친 뒤에, 갈릴리의 자기네 고향 동네 나사렛에 돌아왔다.
v.WLB: 요셉과 마리아는 주의 율법에 따라 모든 일을 마치고 난 뒤 갈릴리에 있는 자기 마을 나사렛으로 돌아왔습니다.

v.CNV: 孩子渐渐长大，强壮起来，充满智慧，有　神的恩典在他身上。
v.ESV: And the child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom. And the favor of God was upon him.
v.GAE: 아기가 자라며 강하여지고 지혜가 충만하며 하나님의 은혜가 그의 위에 있더라
v.KJV: And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit, filled with wisdom: and the grace of God was upon him.
v.NASB: The Child continued to grow and become strong, increasing in wisdom; and the grace of God was upon Him.
v.NIV: And the child grew and became strong; he was filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was on him.
v.NRSV: The child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom; and the favor of God was upon him.
v.SAENEW: 아기는 자라나면서 튼튼해지고, 지혜로 가득 차게 되었고, 또 하나님의 은혜가 그와 함께 하였다.
v.WLB: 아이는 점점 자라 가며 강해지고 지혜가 충만했으며 하나님의 은혜가 그 위에 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 每年逾越节，他父母都上耶路撒冷去。
v.ESV: Now his parents went to Jerusalem every year at the Feast of the Passover.
v.GAE: 그의 부모가 해마다 유월절이 되면 예루살렘으로 가더니
v.KJV: Now his parents went to Jerusalem every year at the feast of the passover.
v.NASB: Now His parents went to Jerusalem every year at the Feast of the Passover.
v.NIV: Every year Jesus’ parents went to Jerusalem for the Festival of the Passover.
v.NRSV: Now every year his parents went to Jerusalem for the festival of the Passover.
v.SAENEW: 예수의 부모는 해마다 유월절에 예루살렘으로 갔다.
v.WLB: 해마다 유월절이 되면 예수의 부모는 예루살렘으로 갔습니다.

v.CNV: 当他十二岁时，他们按着节期的惯例，照常上去。
v.ESV: And when he was twelve years old, they went up according to custom.
v.GAE: 예수께서 열두 살 되었을 때에 그들이 이 절기의 관례를 따라 올라갔다가
v.KJV: And when he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem after the custom of the feast.
v.NASB: And when He became twelve, they went up there according to the custom of the Feast;
v.NIV: When he was twelve years old, they went up to the festival, according to the custom.
v.NRSV: And when he was twelve years old, they went up as usual for the festival.
v.SAENEW: 예수가 열두 살이 되는 해에도, 그들은 절기 관습을 따라 유월절을 지키러 예루살렘에 올라갔다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 열두 살이 되던 해에도 그들은 관례에 따라 절기를 지키러 예루살렘에 올라갔습니다.

v.CNV: 过完了节，他们回去的时候，孩童耶稣仍留在耶路撒冷，他父母却不知道，
v.ESV: And when the feast was ended, as they were returning, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents did not know it,
v.GAE: 그 날들을 마치고 돌아갈 때에 아이 예수는 예루살렘에 머무셨더라 그 부모는 이를 알지 못하고
v.KJV: And when they had fulfilled the days, as they returned, the child Jesus tarried behind in Jerusalem; and Joseph and his mother knew not of it.
v.NASB: and as they were returning, after spending the full number of days, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. But His parents were unaware of it,
v.NIV: After the festival was over, while his parents were returning home, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but they were unaware of it.
v.NRSV: When the festival was ended and they started to return, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but his parents did not know it.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 그들이 절기를 마치고 돌아올 때에, 소년 예수는 예루살렘에 그대로 머물러 있었다. 그의 부모는 이것을 모르고,
v.WLB: 기간이 끝나 그 부모는 집으로 돌아가는 길이었는데 소년 예수는 예루살렘에 남아 있었습니다. 그러나 부모는 이 사실을 알지 못했습니다.

v.CNV: 还以为他在同行的人中间。走了一天，就在亲戚和熟人中找他，
v.ESV: but supposing him to be in the group they went a day's journey, but then they began to search for him among their relatives and acquaintances,
v.GAE: 동행 중에 있는 줄로 생각하고 하룻길을 간 후 친족과 아는 자 중에서 찾되
v.KJV: But they, supposing him to have been in the company, went a day’s journey; and they sought him among their kinsfolk and acquaintance.
v.NASB: but supposed Him to be in the caravan, and went a day's journey; and they began looking for Him among their relatives and acquaintances.
v.NIV: Thinking he was in their company, they traveled on for a day. Then they began looking for him among their relatives and friends.
v.NRSV: Assuming that he was in the group of travelers, they went a day’s journey. Then they started to look for him among their relatives and friends.
v.SAENEW: 일행 가운데 있으려니 생각하고, 하룻길을 갔다. 그 뒤에 비로소 그들의 친척들과 친지들 가운데서 그를 찾았으나,
v.WLB: 그들은 예수가 일행 속에 있으리라 생각하고 하룻길을 가다가 그제야 친척들과 친구들 사이에서 예수를 찾기 시작했습니다.

v.CNV: 没有找到，就转回耶路撒冷找他。
v.ESV: and when they did not find him, they returned to Jerusalem, searching for him.
v.GAE: 만나지 못하매 찾으면서 예루살렘에 돌아갔더니
v.KJV: And when they found him not, they turned back again to Jerusalem, seeking him.
v.NASB: When they did not find Him, they returned to Jerusalem looking for Him.
v.NIV: When they did not find him, they went back to Jerusalem to look for him.
v.NRSV: When they did not find him, they returned to Jerusalem to search for him.
v.SAENEW: 찾지 못하여, 예루살렘으로 되돌아가서 찾아다녔다.
v.WLB: 그러나 찾지 못하자 그들은 예루살렘으로 되돌아가서 예수를 찾았습니다.

v.CNV: 过了三天，才发现他在圣殿里，坐在教师中间，一面听，一面问。
v.ESV: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
v.GAE: 사흘 후에 성전에서 만난즉 그가 선생들 중에 앉으사 그들에게 듣기도 하시며 묻기도 하시니
v.KJV: And it came to pass, that after three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the doctors, both hearing them, and asking them questions.
v.NASB: Then, after three days they found Him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the teachers, both listening to them and asking them questions.
v.NIV: After three days they found him in the temple courts, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
v.NRSV: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
v.SAENEW: 사흘 뒤에야 그들은 성전에서 예수를 찾아냈는데, 그는 선생들 가운데 앉아서, 그들의 말을 듣기도 하고, 그들에게 묻기도 하고 있었다.
v.WLB: 3일이 지나서야 그들은 성전 뜰에서 예수를 찾게 됐습니다. 그는 선생들 가운데 앉아서 이야기를 듣기도 하고 묻기도 하고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 所有听见他的人，都希奇他的聪明和应对。
v.ESV: And all who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.
v.GAE: 듣는 자가 다 그 지혜와 대답을 놀랍게 여기더라
v.KJV: And all that heard him were astonished at his understanding and answers.
v.NASB: And all who heard Him were amazed at His understanding and His answers.
v.NIV: Everyone who heard him was amazed at his understanding and his answers.
v.NRSV: And all who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.
v.SAENEW: 그의 말을 듣고 있던 사람들은 모두 그의 슬기와 대답에 경탄하였다.
v.WLB: 예수의 말을 들은 사람들마다 그가 깨닫고 대답하는 것에 몹시 감탄했습니다.

v.CNV: 他父母见了，非常惊奇，他母亲说：“孩子，为什么这样对待我们呢？你看，你父亲和我都很担心地在找你呢！”
v.ESV: And when his parents saw him, they were astonished. And his mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us so? Behold, your father and I have been searching for you in great distress.”
v.GAE: 그의 부모가 보고 놀라며 그의 어머니는 이르되 아이야 어찌하여 우리에게 이렇게 하였느냐 보라 네 아버지와 내가 근심하여 너를 찾았노라
v.KJV: And when they saw him, they were amazed: and his mother said unto him, Son, why hast thou thus dealt with us? behold, thy father and I have sought thee sorrowing.
v.NASB: When they saw Him, they were astonished; and His mother said to Him, 'Son, why have You treated us this way? Behold, Your father and I have been anxiously looking for You.'
v.NIV: When his parents saw him, they were astonished. His mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us like this? Your father and I have been anxiously searching for you.”
v.NRSV: When his parents saw him they were astonished; and his mother said to him, “Child, why have you treated us like this? Look, your father and I have been searching for you in great anxiety.”
v.SAENEW: 그 부모는 예수를 보고 놀라서, 어머니가 예수에게 말하였다. "얘야, 이게 무슨 일이냐? 네 아버지와 내가 너를 찾느라고 얼마나 애를 태웠는지 모른다."
v.WLB: 그 부모는 예수를 보고 놀랐습니다. 그래서 그의 어머니가 말했습니다. “얘야, 왜 우리에게 이렇게 했느냐? 네 아버지와 내가 얼마나 걱정하며 찾았는지 모른다.”

v.CNV: 他说：“为什么找我呢？你们不知道我必须在我父的家里吗？（“在我父的家里吗？”或译：“以我父的事为念吗？”）”
v.ESV: And he said to them, “Why were you looking for me? Did you not know that I must be in my Father's house?”
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 어찌하여 나를 찾으셨나이까 내가 내 아버지 집에 있어야 될 줄을 알지 못하셨나이까 하시니
v.KJV: And he said unto them, How is it that ye sought me? wist ye not that I must be about my Father’s business?
v.NASB: And He said to them, 'Why is it that you were looking for Me? Did you not know that I had to be in My Father's house?'
v.NIV: “Why were you searching for me?” he asked. “Didn’t you know I had to be in my Father’s house?”
v.NRSV: He said to them, “Why were you searching for me? Did you not know that I must be in my Father’s house?”
v.SAENEW: 예수가 부모에게 말하였다. "어찌하여 나를 찾으셨습니까? 내가 내 아버지의 집에 있어야 할 줄을 알지 못하셨습니까?"
v.WLB: 그러자 예수가 말했습니다. “왜 나를 찾으셨습니까? 내가 마땅히 내 아버지의 집에 있어야 하는 줄 모르셨습니까?”

v.CNV: 但他们不明白他所说的话。
v.ESV: And they did not understand the saying that he spoke to them.
v.GAE: 그 부모가 그가 하신 말씀을 깨닫지 못하더라
v.KJV: And they understood not the saying which he spake unto them.
v.NASB: But they did not understand the statement which He had made to them.
v.NIV: But they did not understand what he was saying to them.
v.NRSV: But they did not understand what he said to them.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 부모는 예수가 자기들에게 한 그 말이 무슨 뜻인지를 깨닫지 못하였다.
v.WLB: 그러나 그들은 예수가 하는 말을 깨닫지 못했습니다.

v.CNV: 他就同他们下去，回到拿撒勒，并且顺从他们。他母亲把这一切事，都存在心里。
v.ESV: And he went down with them and came to Nazareth and was submissive to them. And his mother treasured up all these things in her heart.
v.GAE: 예수께서 함께 내려가사 나사렛에 이르러 순종하여 받드시더라 그 어머니는 이 모든 말을 마음에 두니라
v.KJV: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth, and was subject unto them: but his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.
v.NASB: And He went down with them and came to Nazareth, and He continued in subjection to them; and His mother treasured all these things in her heart.
v.NIV: Then he went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. But his mother treasured all these things in her heart.
v.NRSV: Then he went down with them and came to Nazareth, and was obedient to them. His mother treasured all these things in her heart.
v.SAENEW: 예수는 부모와 함께 내려가 나사렛으로 돌아가서, 그들에게 순종하면서 지냈다. 예수의 어머니는 이 모든 일을 마음에 간직하였다.
v.WLB: 그러고 나서 예수는 부모와 함께 내려가 나사렛으로 돌아가서 부모님께 순종하며 지냈습니다. 예수의 어머니는 이 모든 일을 마음에 간직했습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣的智慧和身量，以及　神和人对他的喜爱，都不断增长。
v.ESV: And Jesus increased in wisdom and in stature and in favor with God and man.
v.GAE: 예수는 지혜와 키가 자라가며 하나님과 사람에게 더욱 사랑스러워 가시더라
v.KJV: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favour with God and man.
v.NASB: And Jesus kept increasing in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.
v.NIV: And Jesus grew in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and man.
v.NRSV: And Jesus increased in wisdom and in years, and in divine and human favor.
v.SAENEW: 예수는 지혜와 키가 자라고, 하나님과 사람에게 더욱 사랑을 받았다.
v.WLB: 그리고 예수는 지혜와 키가 점점 더 자라 가며 하나님과 사람들로부터 사랑을 받았습니다.
